---
title: Project Description
author:
  - Solveig Røndal-Liniger (szc836)
  - Oliver Eiberg Jørgensen (zkt394)
date: 2026-04-23
date-format: MMMM DD, YYYY
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false
format:
  pdf: 
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    geometry:
      - top=30mm
      - left=25mm
      - bottom=25mm
      - right=25mm
      - includefoot=false
    lang: en
    fontsize: 12pt
    highlight-style: github-light
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \setstretch{1.3}
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.1ex,afterskip=0.01ex]{section}
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.1ex,afterskip=0.01ex]{subsection}
        \RedeclareSectionCommand[beforeskip=0.1ex,afterskip=0.01ex]{subsubsection}
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \usepackage{graphicx}
        \usepackage{eso-pic}
        \pagestyle{fancy}
        \setlength{\footskip}{36pt}
        \fancyhf{}
        \lfoot{\shortstack[l]{{\bfseries University of Copenhagen}\\Department of Economics}}
        \rfoot{\thepage}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
        \renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0pt}
        \fancypagestyle{plain}{%
          \fancyhf{}
          \lfoot{\shortstack[l]{{\bfseries University of Copenhagen}\\Department of Economics}}
          \rfoot{\thepage}
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          \renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0pt}
        }
        \AddToShipoutPictureFG{%
          \AtPageUpperLeft{%
            \put(\LenToUnit{\paperwidth-10mm-5cm},\LenToUnit{-2.5cm}){%
              \includegraphics[width=5cm]{ku_logo_uk_h.png}%
            }%
          }%
        }
---

<!-- Importing libraries -->
```{python}
# Loading necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from plotnine.geoms.geom import geom
import tidyfinance as tf
from plotnine import *
from mizani.formatters import percent_format
from scipy.optimize import minimize
from IPython.display import Latex
import pandas as pd
# Set seed for reproducibility (used in exercise 5)
np.random.seed(2026)
```
\newpage

## Project Description {.unnumbered}

In this term paper, we will extend the model developed by Aguirregabria (2023) by incorporating the possibility of purchasing multiple units of a storable good at time...
Does allowing for quantity choice improve the modeling of forward-looking demand by capturing dynamic information beyond duration since last purchase and brand switching costs?


Our key reference is Aquirregabria (2023) who develops a dynamic demand model for differentaited products with fixed-effects unobserved heterogeneity. The model differs to other papers buy modelling unobserved heterogenity with fixed effects as opposed to random effects. The model
